business.headline: Govt rules out using unclaimed LIC, EPFO funds for other purposesThe government told the Lok Sabha there is no proposal to divert unclaimed LIC funds or EPFO inoperative account money. It said the existing transfer and settlement mechanisms will continue while outreach to claimants is stepped up.

US-India trade deal likely in 3-4 months, says senior US officialA senior US official has said that negotiations between the two countries are almost complete, with only a few procedural steps remaining before the deal can be finalised.

Middle East conflict could drag global growth to 1.3% in 2026, warns World BankAccording to World Bank Chief Economist Indermit Gill, if hostilities continue for several more months, the world could face slower growth, higher inflation and rising borrowing costs.

Sensex ends 715 points lower, Nifty below 24,000; IndiGo down 4%The BSE Sensex fell 715.06 points, or 0.92%, to close at 76,755.05, while the NSE Nifty50 declined 191.45 points, or 0.79%, to settle at 23,996.25, slipping below the key 24,000 mark.
Can you settle pending PF damages at lower rates? This EPFO scheme can helpOne of the biggest attractions of the Vishwas 2026 Scheme is the reduced rate of penal damages.

India may open skies to new airlines in bid to challenge IndiGo-Air India duopolyIndia is considering easing rules to let airport operators such as Adani and GMR run airlines. The move is aimed at widening competition in a domestic market dominated by IndiGo and Air India.

Property tax rules, AIS errors and more: 6 biggest ITR filing challenges this yearITR filing may be digital and largely pre-filled, but taxpayers are still running into several roadblocks. I spoke to tax experts to find out where taxpayers are making the most mistakes this filing season. Are you making one of them too?

Why are your foreign bank accounts and investments showing in AIS?Did you log into your Annual Information Statement (AIS) and notice your foreign bank account or overseas investments listed there? Wondering how the Income Tax Department got those details and what it means for your ITR filing?

Lupin, Aurobindo, Sun Pharma fall up to 4%: Why are pharma stocks falling today?Dalal Street fell sharply after Donald Trump announced a phased tariff plan for imported generic medicines. The proposal has raised concerns over costs, manufacturing shifts and the long-term outlook for Indian drugmakers.

Sensex tanks 700 points: 3 reasons why the stock market is falling todayBenchmark indices extended their losses in afternoon trade, with the BSE Sensex tumbling 746.20 points, or 0.96%, to 76,723.91, while the NSE Nifty50 fell 198.55 points, or 0.82%, to slip below the key 24,000 mark and trade at 23,989.15 around 12:24 pm.

Sensex, Nifty open lower as Brent crude climbs above $92The BSE Sensex fell 367.92 points, or 0.47%, to 77,102.19, while the NSE Nifty50 declined 99.10 points, or 0.41%, to 24,088.60 at around 9:20 am.

Stock market today: Will Sensex, Nifty fall as crude crosses $92 mark?GIFT Nifty futures were trading around 24,117 at 7:56 am IST, indicating that the Nifty50 could open below Tuesday's close of 24,187.70.

Are you eligible for additional UPS benefits? Here's what govt saysReplying to a question in the Lok Sabha, Minister of State for Finance Pankaj Chaudhary said that 25,756 retired Central government subscribers are eligible to claim additional benefits under the UPS.

Volkswagen weighs JSW partnership for India businessVolkswagen is in advanced talks with JSW Group over an investment in Skoda Auto Volkswagen India. The proposed deal could reshape the carmaker's India strategy as competition intensifies.

Everyone wants AI engineers. But who will make sure AI actually works?As companies increasingly deploy AI across banking, healthcare, customer service and other sectors, another talent shortage may be quietly emerging; professionals who can test whether these systems are accurate, secure and reliable before they reach users.

Maruti Suzuki announces fresh price hike: Check how much more you'll have to payMaruti Suzuki will raise vehicle prices by up to Rs 30,000 from August. The move marks its second portfolio-wide increase in about two months amid sustained cost pressures.

Can ITR portal handle the filing rush before July 31? Here's what government saysIn a written reply in Parliament, the government said that while some users may experience occasional delays during periods of heavy traffic, the portal has remained largely stable despite a significant increase in the number of taxpayers using it this year.

RBI's foreign currency deposit scheme for NRIs explainedThe RBI has rolled out a concessional swap facility to revive FCNR(B) deposits after a sharp fall in inflows. The move could help banks raise rates, but NRIs still need to weigh currency needs and taxes.

Dalal Street ends lower on HDFC Bank drag; midcaps continue to outperformThe BSE Sensex closed 238.41 points, or 0.31%, lower at 77,470.11, while the NSE Nifty50 fell 50.80 points, or 0.21%, to settle at 24,187.70.

Gift Deed vs Sale Deed vs Will: Here's how to pick the right way to transfer propertyA single document can decide how smoothly your property changes hands and whether your family faces disputes later. So, should you opt for a Gift Deed, Sale Deed or a Will?

entertainment.headline: ZEE5 announces series on NEET paper leak amid students' protest at Jantar Mantar An OTT series titled The Scam: Leaked has been announced as protests over examination irregularities widen. Its teaser arrives amid student unrest, celebrity support and calls for education reforms.

Anupam Kher backs students, warns against political opportunists hijacking protest Anupam Kher backed students protesting over the NEET paper leak and education reform. He urged them to keep the movement free of political opportunists as the Jantar Mantar protest continued.

Jana Nayagan producer upset over A certificate, cites girl child empowerment theme Producer Venkat K Narayana said Jana Nayagan's A certificate was a disappointment after the makers hoped for a U/A rating. He said the decision limits the film's reach among families despite its message on girl child empowerment.

I wanted Punjab to feel lived-in, not an outsider's view: Ab Hisaab Hoga director Ab Hisaab Hoga director Divyanshu said he worked to make Punjab feel authentic in the series. He said the show's workshops and rooted details reflect why OTT should keep backing smaller stories.

R Madhavan to host OTT reboot of Farooq Shaikh's talk show Jeena Isi Ka Naam Hai Actor R Madhavan will host the OTT return of Jeena Isi Ka Naam Hai, taking over from late actor Farooq Shaikh. Madhavan said the reboot will honour the show's legacy of empathy, sincerity and personal storytelling.

Can Jana Nayagan beat the leak? Trade predicts Vijay's box office fate Trade voices are divided over CM Vijay's Jana Nayagan's opening after its leak and delayed release. Strong advance bookings continue, but piracy and the A certificate may affect repeat footfalls.

Ryan Reynolds confirms return as Wade Wilson in new Deadpool film Actor Ryan Reynolds confirmed another Deadpool film is in the works at the Fanatics Fest 2026. Reynolds had last featured alongside actor Hugh Jackman in Marvel Studios' Deadpool & Wolverine.

Anushka Sharma, Virat Kohli make another spiritual visit to Premanand Maharaj. Watch Anushka Sharma and Virat Kohli visited Premanand Maharaj's Radha Keli Kunj Ashram in Vrindavan on Wednesday morning. The clips surfaced a month after their previous visit and reflected the couple's regular return to the ashram.

Lock Upp: Harshad Chopda, Shivangi Joshi slam Shilpa Shinde over lovebirds taunt Harshad Chopda and Shivangi Joshi confronted Shilpa Shinde on Lock Upp after she repeatedly called them 'lovebirds' during an argument over a gym mat. Shinde had previously raised questions about Joshi's character on the show.

Ratna Pathak Shah backs students' protest, questions India's 'Vishwaguru' claim Ratna Pathak Shah voiced support for students protesting at Jantar Mantar over education reform. Invoking Eklavya, she said the system was taking away young people's future and added to wider backing for the agitation.

Lyricist Manoj Muntashir calls Jantar Mantar protesters part of 'tukde tukde gang' Lyricist Manoj Muntashir backed students over the alleged NEET paper leak but refused to join the Jantar Mantar protest. Calling the leak indefensible and demanding strict accountability, he mentioned that he won't stand alongside the "tukde tukde gang."

Rapper Arivu, detained for Chennai NEET protest, clarifies he did not meet CM Vijay Tamil rapper Arivu said he met minister Aadhav Arjuna at the Tamil Nadu Secretariat and not Chief Minister Vijay. The clarification followed reports about his detention during an anti-NEET protest and his appeal for support to students.

Spooky in Love first impression: Park Eun-bin elevates a familiar ghostly romance Spooky in Love pairs ghost-seeing hotel CEO Cheon Yeo-ri with prosecutor Ma Gang-wook in a light paranormal romance. Park Eun-bin's turn gives the familiar setup enough charm to hold attention.

They fear Punjab will unite: Satluj director Honey Trehan on film's ban in India Filmmaker Honey Trehan said the controversy around Satluj began with fears that the film could unite Punjab. He said its screenings after the ZEE5 takedown challenge arguments about law and order, security and censorship.

Young people bleeding on the road: Actor Amol Parashar recalls CJP protest horror Amol Parashar has shared his experience of attending CJP's Chalo Sansad march, saying the sight of "young people bleeding on the road" continues to haunt him. The actor recalled crying after witnessing the police action and praised the students for their empathy, resilience and unity.

Growth is fair education: Rajkummar Rao on CJP's students protest, police violence Actor Rajkummar Rao urged peace, dialogue and compassion as the Cockroach Janata Party-led student protest widened in Delhi. His appeal came amid police violence allegations, celebrity backing and continued demands for exam reform.

Lock Upp 2: Dheeraj Dhoopar's wife Vinny Arora backs him after chit controversy Actor Dheeraj Dhoopar's family meeting on Lock Upp 2 came under scrutiny after chits were allegedly sent inside. His actor-wife Vinny Arora called it an emotional mistake, while viewers questioned the show's lenient response.

Vijay's Jana Nayagan mints Rs 20 crore in advance bookings, 7 lakh tickets sold Jana Nayagan has crossed Rs 20 crore in domestic advance bookings, including blocked seats, ahead of release. The strong pre-sales have kept trade hopeful despite delays, an HD leak and the film trailing Vijay's last two openers.

Chauhaan: Zeeshan Ayyub distances himself, says his voice was misused in teaser Actor Mohammed Zeeshan Ayyub said he is not part of Chauhaan and had dubbed only one line without any reference. His clarification came after the teaser drew objections and his voice was replaced following his protest.

general news.headline: Education system rigged: Rahul Gandhi cites 152 paper leaks, zero convictions Live updatesAlleging that India's education system had become a "rigged system", Congress MP Rahul Gandhi attacked the government, asserting that 152 paper leaks occurred in the past decade without a single conviction.

Nepal PM Balen Shah's big climbdown, will meet India and China envoys separatelyAfter months of shunning one-on-one meetings with foreign diplomats and drawing criticism at home, Nepal PM Balen Shah is set to break his own self-imposed rule. He said he would be holding separate talks with the envoys of India and China.

IPL founder Lalit Modi set to return to India after 16 yearsIPL founder and former chairman Lalit Modi has said he will return to India after 16 years following a tribunal verdict that gave him major relief in the 2009 IPL South Africa Foreign Exchange Management Act (FEMA) case.

How Rahul Gandhi launched a protest, without joining a protestCongress leader Rahul Gandhi didn't join the Cockroach Janta Party's protest at Jantar Mantar. Instead, he started one of his own. Here's why India's main opposition party chose a parallel agitation instead of standing with the student-led movement already on the streets.

Did Dharmendra Pradhan lead a 1997 paper leak protest?Protests over alleged exam irregularities have once again put the spotlight on the Union Education Minister, social media has unearthed an old claim from his student politics days. A 2021 article recounts his participation in a 1997 protest linked to a paper leak...

Live: 'Sarkar hataiye,' cops whispered as they dragged me away, says Rahul GandhiCJP Protest in Delhi Live Updates: Protesters of the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) reclaimed Delhi's Jantar Mantar on Tuesday morning after police dismantled the protest stage and tents overnight following a day of clashes between demonstrators and security personnel. Heavy deployment of Delhi Police and Rapid Action Force (RAF) personnel remained in place around the protest site. Leader of the Opposition Rahul Gandhi, whose absence from the protest had drawn attention as several opposition leaders and prominent citizens thronged the site, later led a group of Congress MPs in a sit-in outside the Prime Minister's residence. This led to dramatic scenes as police removed Gandhi and other lawmakers from the area. Gandhi was briefly detained before being released. Tensions escalated again late Tuesday when stone-pelting was reported on Delhi Police and RAF personnel in Connaught Place, a short distance from Jantar Mantar. Stay with Indiatoday.in for latest updates

Sonam Wangchuk says he will end fast only if govt assures no punitive action against student protestersSonam Wangchuk says he will end fast only if govt assures no punitive action against student protesters

Mamata cuts short Kalyan Banerjee's Martyrs' Day speech, TMC MP walks outA nearly 23-minute speech by Kalyan Banerjee triggered a public spat with Mamata Banerjee at the Trinamool's Martyrs' Day rally, ending in his walkout. While Kalyan denied exceeding his allotted time and said he was "extremely hurt," party sources blamed the delay for disrupting the programme.

Skyroot CEO recalls phone call that brought father to tears after Vikram-1 successAfter Vikram-1 reached orbit, Skyroot Aerospace CEO Pawan Kumar Chandana said his first call was to his father. Recalling his reaction, Chandana said the conversation was filled with silence and emotion.

How a bloodied Rahul Gandhi gave Congress the image it wantedRahul Gandhi and senior Congress leaders were detained outside the Prime Minister's residence during a flash protest on Tuesday. It helped Congress reclaim the space over the paper leak issue while escalating pressure on the Modi government.

Have govt, Congress struck a deal? Akhilesh Yadav fumes over NEET discussionSamajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav objected in the Lok Sabha after he was not allowed to speak on the NEET-UG paper leak and the crackdown on the July 20 youth protest.

Why cops wanted to make AI chatbot an accomplice in Bengaluru triple murderA triple murder in Bengaluru has become more than just another crime story. Bengaluru Police allege that the accused spent six months consulting Google's Gemini chatbot to plan the killings, making it one of their first murder probes in which AI is suspected to have played such an extensive role.

India has a new Chinese headache, it is called KimiThe world is fast turning into a space divided between those who have access to top AI and those who don't, similar to how some nations have 5th-generation fighter planes and most don't. The US has Mythos, China now has Kimi, and that could be a headache for India.

J&K cop on patrol shot dead in Anantnag, Lashkar proxy claims responsibilitySoon after the attack, security forces cordoned off the area and launched a massive search and counter-terror operation to trace the assailant.

5 systems over Bay of Bengal, Arabian Sea revive monsoon rain, Isro images showIsro's INSAT-3DR and INSAT-3DS satellites have captured five cyclonic circulations over the Bay of Bengal and Arabian Sea feeding an active monsoon trough, bringing rain back to Delhi and central India. Here is what the images show and why the science works.

Khichdi failed: Why Nasa cleared just three space foods prepared by DRDOShubhanshu Shukla said DRDO prepared several Indian dishes for his ISS mission, but only three were cleared for flight. The episode highlights the strict testing and time pressures shaping India's space food plans for Gaganyaan.

Cockroaches using apps that send messages without internet? Here is tech behind itWhile Delhi Police seems to be implementing an internet blackout by cutting off 4G and 5G access in central Delhi areas, it seems Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) protesters are using some specialised apps to send messages without internet. The tech is real, and here is how it works by relying on Bluetooth.

25 days of fast, 10 minutes with government: Inside Wangchuk's night at MedantaSonam Wangchuk was shifted from Safdarjung Hospital to Medanta after winning a High Court order allowing treatment at a hospital of his choice during the 25th day of his hunger strike.

Sensex tanks 700 points: 3 reasons why the stock market is falling todayBenchmark indices extended their losses in afternoon trade, with the BSE Sensex tumbling 746.20 points, or 0.96%, to 76,723.91, while the NSE Nifty50 fell 198.55 points, or 0.82%, to slip below the key 24,000 mark and trade at 23,989.15 around 12:24 pm.

CJP appeals to supporters to stop sending food to protest sites as supplies pile upFood deliveries poured into Delhi's Jantar Mantar from all over the country until Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) organisers urged supporters to stop ordering more.

Indian woman shows you why life in US comes with absolutely 'no princess treatment'In a video titled "Life in USA," an Indian woman documented herself filling a large water container at a dispenser, inserting money into the machine and then wheeling the heavy jar back home on a trolley.

Surat Police bust AI-powered cyber fraud network run by 18-year-old dropoutDespite dropping out after Class 11, police said Rohit Virendrasinh Shakya became an expert coder by the age of 16 and used AI tools and Telegram channels to develop fake applications that closely resembled genuine apps. He operated on a subscription model, selling the malicious APK files to cybercriminals for Rs 15,000 a month.

Pakistan seeks $10 billion US currency backstop after Iran war diplomacyPakistan has sought a USD 10 billion exchange stabilisation facility from the United States. The request could ease pressure on reserves and the rupee while signalling closer ties with Washington.

Don't have time to watch videos: CJI on plea against crackdown on CJP protestersChief Justice Surya Kant declined to urgently hear a petition over "police brutality" against students protesting at Jantar Mantar. "Don't waste our time," the CJI told the lawyer who mentioned the matter.

sports.headline: IPL founder Lalit Modi set to return to India after 16 years IPL founder and former chairman Lalit Modi has said he will return to India after 16 years following a tribunal verdict that gave him major relief in the 2009 IPL South Africa Foreign Exchange Management Act (FEMA) case.

Sindhu wildly celebrates win vs Unnati Hooda, shares cryptic social media post China Open 2026: A huge roar on court, followed by a cryptic message off it. PV Sindhu battled past Unnati Hooda in three games before sharing a social media post that sparked plenty of intrigue.

UTT: Kolkata edge U Mumba in thriller as both teams seal semis spot Kolkata Thunder Blades ended defending champions U Mumba TT's winning streak with a dramatic 8-7 victory to secure a place in the UTT Season 7 semi-finals, with both teams advancing to the knockout stage.

Benched in England, Kuldeep Yadav signs for Yorkshire for one-dayers and County Kuldeep Yadav has joined Yorkshire after being left out of India's ODI XI in England. The stint gives him immediate match time before the Sri Lanka Tests and a county run with the English club later in the year.

Judge us after: Fulton, tactical discipline, and India's quest to exorcise 1975 For Craig Fulton, the aim at the World Cup is to end a 51-year-old semi-final curse for the Indian team. With this goal firmly in his mind, the Indian coach has decided to go for a team that will fit into his mantra of tactical discipline, structure and long-term stability.

ICC ODI rankings: Rohit Sharma closes on Virat Kohli as No. 1 battle heats up Rohit Sharma gained 14 rating points after his hundred at Lord's on Saturday. Three of the top four in the ICC rankings for men's ODIs are Indians. Shubman Gill is closing on in No. 1 Daryl Mitchell.

Ben Stokes battles cramps, scores first hundred after international retirement Ben Stokes returned for Durham and made an unbeaten 108 against Derbyshire despite severe cramps. The innings underlined his resilience and gave Durham a five-wicket win days after his Test retirement call.

Embracing the rollercoaster, Sooryavanshi returns to favourite hunting ground Zimbabwe vs India:Vaibhav Sooryavanshi returns to Harare for India's Zimbabwe T20Is after a testing debut in England. The 15-year-old hopes familiar conditions help convert promise into a first senior impact.

Scaloni rejects unfair refereeing chat: Spain were better in World Cup final Lionel Scaloni rejected complaints about the refereeing after Argentina's World Cup final defeat to Spain. He said Spain were the better side, while admitting that fatigue may have also been a reason behind the loss.

Hasmatullah Shahidi steps down as Afghanistan ODI captain historic 4-year run Hashmatullah Shahidi has stepped down as Afghanistan's ODI captain but remains available for selection. His exit closes a notable phase that included a near semi-final World Cup run in 2023 and opens the search for a new skipper before the Ireland series.

David Warner pleads guilty to drink driving, faces up to 9 months in jail David Warner pleaded guilty to a mid-range drink-driving charge in Sydney through his lawyer. The conviction could bring jail time and trigger a review of his Sydney Thunder captaincy.

Rohit Sharma proves Rohit Sharma isn't India's ODI problemRohit Sharma answered the only question worth asking of him at Lord's. What India actually need answered are the harder ones: a bowling attack built around one man's fitness, a potential lead spinner being overlooked, and search for all-rounders without a plan.

Did FIFA let Argentina's Leandro Paredes off the hook after World Cup final brawl?Leandro Paredes' red card after the brawl following the World Cup final has been deleted by FIFA. Paredes was seen coming to blows with Eric Garcia and Gavi after the end of the game.

Chelsea take sly dig at Arsenal after completing record Morgan Rogers dealChelsea completed a record British deal to sign Morgan Rogers from Aston Villa after beating Arsenal to him. The Blues decided to a cheeky dig at their London rivals after Rogers picked them over the current Premier League champions.

Think India will bludgeon past Zimbabwe in the T20I series? Think againZIM vs IND: India may be the more experienced side heading into the T20I series, but Zimbabwe can be a dangerous proposition at home and should not be underestimated.

Tilak Varma gives perfect response to strike-rate critics. Here's what he saidTilak Varma pushed back against questions over his reduced strike-rate before the Zimbabwe series. Tilak has struggled to maintain a consistent strike-rate over the last couple of years in T20Is.

Fresh off Lord's heroics, Rohit Sharma arrives in style for The Hundred openerRohit Sharma turned up at the Oval for The Hundred opener after his fighting 138 at Lord's and was seen with fans before an intense chat with Nita Ambani. The visit for the match came on the heels of his hundred against England, which effectively shut down rumours of retirement.

La Liga chief accuses FIFA of destroying football, calls for Infantino to resignLa Liga president Javier Tebas accused FIFA of "destroying the football industry" through the expansion of international competitions and said president Gianni Infantino should resign.

India boxing team hit by luggage chaos ahead of Commonwealth Games campaignIndia's boxing contingent suffered an early setback to its Commonwealth Games campaign on Tuesday after nearly half the team's luggage failed to arrive from Belfast.

Football's strangest red card? Player sent-off for urinating next to benchBrazilian forward Micael was shown a rare red card before kick-off in the Copa Paulista after urinating beside the substitutes' bench. However, the team did not have to play with a man short.

Chennai Grand Masters: D Gukesh suffers second loss, Sarin beats ErigaiseWorld champion D Gukesh suffered his second consecutive defeat at the Chennai Grand Masters after losing to GM M Pranesh, while GM Nihal Sarin stunned Arjun Erigaisi in the penultimate round on Tuesday.

When in doubt, talk to Ashish Nehra: Ashok Sharma ahead of potential India debutIndia fast bowler Ashok Sharma credited Gujarat Titans head coach Ashish Nehra for his rapid rise, revealing the former India pacer was always available for advice as he prepares for a likely T20I debut against Zimbabwe.

Rohit Sharma's retirement speculation wasn't merely outside noise: AshwinFormer India spinner Ravichandran Ashwin believes there was substance to the speculation surrounding Rohit Sharma's ODI future. Speaking on his YouTube channel, Ashwin said that there is no smoke without fire.

Pressure is intense: Referee Anthony Taylor retires from football officiatingReferee Anthony Taylor has announced his retirement from football officiating with immediate effect, saying the intense pressure and constant scrutiny of the job played a key role in his decision.

Murali Sreeshankar eyes Glasgow gold after strong injury comebackIndia's premier long jumper Murali Sreeshankar believes his finest performances are still ahead of him as he prepares for the Commonwealth Games in Glasgow, saying a "big jump" is around the corner after making an impressive return from a career-threatening injury.

India's recent T20I slump due to overplaying in Asia, says Tilak VarmaVice-captain Tilak Varma addressed India's recent T20I slump during the pre-series press conference in Harare. Varma argued that the team lost all six T20Is in the UK because they spent the majority of the last two years playing in Asia.

BCCI cracks down: Deliberate front-foot no-balls to invite match suspensionThe BCCI has revised its domestic playing conditions, introducing match-long suspensions for deliberate front-foot no-balls and non-landing deliveries. The changes also include revisions to end-of-day play, final-innings declarations and wicketkeeping rules ahead of the new domestic season.

India tour still on? Bangladesh hopeful despite Mustafizur Rahman IPL sagaBangladesh remains hopeful of hosting India in September and believes sports diplomacy can help ease tensions sparked by the Mustafizur Rahman controversy.

Vinicius Jr. gets face surgery, looks unrecognisable after FIFA World CupBrazil star Vinicius Jr. has reportedly undergone a chin harmonisation procedure after the FIFA World Cup. Images of his transformed appearance are going viral ahead of his return to Real Madrid.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is special, manage him well: Sikandar Raza to IndiaZIM vs IND: Zimbabwe captain Sikandar Raza has urged India to carefully manage teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, backing the 15-year-old to become a generational talent if nurtured properly.

Prannoy, Devika knocked out as India make disappointing China Open startHS Prannoy and Devika Sihag suffered first-round exits at the China Open as India's campaign got off to a disappointing start. PV Sindhu, Lakshya Sen and Ayush Shetty begin their campaigns on Wednesday.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
